list.title: BGE.Canvas
list.description: Creates a Canvas object and bitmap

list.title: BGE.Canvas#clear
list.description: Clears the canvas to the given background color

list.title: BGE.Canvas#getHeight
list.description: Gets the height of the underlying bitmap

list.title: BGE.Canvas#getOffset
list.description: Gets the offset of the canvas from the screen

list.title: BGE.Canvas#getScale
list.description: Gets the scale of the canvas

list.title: BGE.Canvas#getWidth
list.description: Gets the width of the underlying bitmap

list.title: BGE.Canvas#setOffset
list.description: Sets the offset of the canvas. This is as Float to allow incrementing by less than 1 pixel, it is converted to integer internally

list.title: BGE.Canvas#setScale
list.description: Sets the scale of the canvas

list.title: BGE.Canvas#setSize
list.description: Changes the size of the canvas

list.title: BGE.CircleCollider
list.description: Create a new CircleCollider

list.title: BGE.CircleCollider#debugDraw
list.description: Draws the circle outline around the collider

list.title: BGE.CircleCollider#refreshColliderRegion
list.description: Refreshes the collider

list.title: BGE.Collider
list.description: Creates a new Collider

list.title: BGE.Collider#adjustCompositorObject
list.description: Moves the compositor to the new x,y position - called from Game when the entity it is attached to moves

list.title: BGE.Collider#debugDraw
list.description: Helper function to draw an outline around the collider

list.title: BGE.Collider#refreshColliderRegion
list.description: Refreshes the collider. Called every frame by the GameEngine. Should be overrided by sub classes if they have specialized collision set ups (e.g. circle, rectangle).

list.title: BGE.Collider#setupCompositor
list.description: Sets up this collider to be associated with a given game and entity

list.title: BGE.Drawable#forceRedraw
list.description: Forces a redraw of this drawable on next frame By default, some drawables that need to do preprocessing (text, polygons, etc) will only redraw automatically if their dimensions or underlying values change --

list.title: BGE.Drawable#setCanvas
list.description: Sets the canvas this will draw to

list.title: BGE.DrawablePolygon#drawTriangleEdges
list.description: Draw a triangle edges - uses outlineRGBA if drawOutline is true

list.title: BGE.DrawablePolygon#fillPolygonWithRectangles
list.description: Draw a filled convex polygon with rectangles

list.title: BGE.DrawablePolygon#fillTriangleWithRays
list.description: Draw a filled triangle with rays from the apex ato the shortest side of the triangle

list.title: BGE.Game
list.description: Constructor for GameEngine

list.title: BGE.Game#End
list.description: Ends the Game

list.title: BGE.Game#Pause
list.description: Pauses the game Only entities marked as pausable = false will be processed in game loop For each entity, the onPause() function will be called

list.title: BGE.Game#Play
list.description: Starts the game engine. Run this function after setting up the game.

list.title: BGE.Game#Resume
list.description: Resumes / unpauses the game For each entity, the onResume() function will be called, and any image in the entity will have its onResume() called

list.title: BGE.Game#addEntity
list.description: Adds a game entity to be processed by the game engine Only entities that have been added will be part of the game Calls the entity's onCreate() function with the args provided

list.title: BGE.Game#centerCanvasToScreen
list.description: Centers the canvas on the screen

list.title: BGE.Game#changeRoom
list.description: Changes the active room to the one with the given name Calls the room's onCreate() method with the args given TODO: work on rooms

list.title: BGE.Game#debugDrawColliders
list.description: Set if colliders should be drawn

list.title: BGE.Game#debugDrawEntityDetails
list.description: Set GameEntity and SceneObject debug view on or off

list.title: BGE.Game#debugDrawSafeZones
list.description: Set if Safe Zone should be drawn

list.title: BGE.Game#debugLimitFrameRate
list.description: Limit the frame rate to the given number of frames per second

list.title: BGE.Game#debugSetColors
list.description: Sets the colors for the debug items to be drawn colors = {colliders: integer, safe_action_zone: integer, safe_title_zone: integer}

list.title: BGE.Game#debugShowUi
list.description: Set if Debug UI/Windows should be drawn

list.title: BGE.Game#defineInterface
list.description: TODO: work on interfaces

list.title: BGE.Game#defineRoom
list.description: Defines a room (like a level) in the game TODO: work on rooms

list.title: BGE.Game#destroyAllEntities
list.description: Destroys all entities with a given name

list.title: BGE.Game#destroyEntity
list.description: Destroys an entity and all its colliders Clears its properties, so images, etc. won't get drawn anymore

list.title: BGE.Game#entityCount
list.description: Gets the number of entities of a given name

list.title: BGE.Game#fitCanvasToScreen
list.description: Scales and positions the current canvas to fit the screen

list.title: BGE.Game#get3dModel
list.description: Gets a 3d Model (Model3d) by the name given to it when load3dModel() was called

list.title: BGE.Game#getAllEntities
list.description: Gets all the entities that match the given name

list.title: BGE.Game#getAllEntitiesWithInterface
list.description: TODO: work on interfaces

list.title: BGE.Game#getBitmap
list.description: Gets a bitmap image (roBitmap) by the name given to it when loadBitmap() was called

list.title: BGE.Game#getCanvas
list.description: Gets the bitmap the game is currently drawing to

list.title: BGE.Game#getDebugUI
list.description: Gets the main debug window to add other debug widgets to

list.title: BGE.Game#getDeltaTime
list.description: What's the time in seconds since last frame?

list.title: BGE.Game#getEmptyBitmap
list.description: Gets a 1x1 bitmap image (used for collider compositing)

list.title: BGE.Game#getEntityByID
list.description: Gets an entity by its unique id

list.title: BGE.Game#getEntityByName
list.description: Gets the first entity with the given name

list.title: BGE.Game#getFont
list.description: Gets a font object, to be used for writing text to the screen For example, in BGE.DrawText()

list.title: BGE.Game#getGarbageCollectionStats
list.description: Gets the latest stats from automatic garbage collection https://developer.roku.com/en-ca/docs/references/brightscript/language/global-utility-functions.md#rungarbagecollector-as-object

list.title: BGE.Game#getNextGameEntityId
list.description: Gets the next valid id for a GameEntity

list.title: BGE.Game#getRoom
list.description: Gets the current Room/Level the game is using

list.title: BGE.Game#getScreen
list.description: Gets the screen object

list.title: BGE.Game#getTotalTime
list.description: What's the total time in seconds since ths start

list.title: BGE.Game#getUI
list.description: Gets the UI Container to add new UI elements (which get drawn on top off Game Entities)

list.title: BGE.Game#isPaused
list.description: Is the game paused?

list.title: BGE.Game#load3dModel
list.description: Loads an image file (png/jpg) to be used as an image in the game

list.title: BGE.Game#loadBitmap
list.description: Loads an image file (png/jpg) to be used as an image in the game

list.title: BGE.Game#loadFont
list.description: Loads a font from the registry, and assigns it the given name

list.title: BGE.Game#loadSound
list.description: Loads a sound file from the given path to be played later

list.title: BGE.Game#musicPause
list.description: Pauses the currently playing music

list.title: BGE.Game#musicPlay
list.description: Plays an audio file at the given path This is designed for music, where only one file can play at a time.

list.title: BGE.Game#musicResume
list.description: Resumes / unpauses the current music

list.title: BGE.Game#musicStop
list.description: Stops the currently playing music file

list.title: BGE.Game#newAsyncUrlTransfer
list.description: Creates a new URL Async Transfer object, which is handled by the game loop Events from this URL transfer will be set to entities via the onUrlEvent() method

list.title: BGE.Game#playSound
list.description: Plays the given sound

list.title: BGE.Game#postGameEvent
list.description: General purpose event dispatch method Game entities can listen for events via the onGameEvent() method

list.title: BGE.Game#raycastAngle
list.description: Performs a raycast from a certain location along a n angle to find any colliders on that line

list.title: BGE.Game#raycastVector
list.description: Performs a raycast from a certain location along a vector to find any colliders on that line

list.title: BGE.Game#registerFont
list.description: Registers a font by its path

list.title: BGE.Game#resetRoom
list.description: Resets the current room to its state at when it was first created

list.title: BGE.Game#resetScreen
list.description: Resets the screen Note: Important This function is here because of a bug with the Roku. If you ever try to use a component that displays something on the screen aside from roScreen, such as roKeyboardScreen, roMessageDialog, etc. the screen will flicker after you return to your game You should always call this method after using a screen that's outside of roScreen in order to prevent this bug.

list.title: BGE.Game#setBackgroundColor
list.description: Sets the default background color for the game Before any entities are drawn, the screen is cleared to this color

list.title: BGE.Game#setInputEntity
list.description: Set only one entity to receive onInput() calls Useful for when a menu/pause screen should handle all input

list.title: BGE.Game#unload3dModel
list.description: Invalidates a 3d Model name, so it can't be loaded again

list.title: BGE.Game#unloadBitmap
list.description: Invalidates a bitmap name, so it can't be loaded again

list.title: BGE.Game#unloadFont
list.description: Unloads a font so it can't be used again

list.title: BGE.Game#unsetInputEntity
list.description: Unset that only one entity will receive onInputCalls()

list.title: BGE.GameEntity
list.description: Creates a new GameEntity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addAnimatedImage
list.description: Adds a animated image to be drawn for this entity. Animated images cycle through regions of a bitmap (e.g. spritesheet)

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addCircleCollider
list.description: Adds a circle collider to this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addCollider
list.description: Adds a collider that has already been constructed

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addImage
list.description: Adds a basic image (non-animated) to be drawn for this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addImageObject
list.description: Adds any Image object to this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addInterface

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addRectangleCollider
list.description: Adds a rectangle collider to this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#addSprite

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#clearAllColliders
list.description: Remove all colliders from this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#debugDraw
list.description: Draws the entity's axes and/or its name

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#getCollider
list.description: Gets a collider based on its name

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#getImage
list.description: Gets an image by its name from the lookup table

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#getStaticVariable

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#hasInterface
list.description: TODO: work on Interfaces

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#invalidate
list.description: Marks this entity as invalid, so it will be cleared/destroyed at the end of the frame

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#isValid
list.description: Is this still a valid entity?

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onAudioEvent
list.description: Method to handle audio events

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onChangeRoom
list.description: Method called when the current room changes. This method is only called when the entity is marked as persistant, otherwise entities are destroyed on room changes.

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onCollision
list.description: Method for processing all collisions

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onCreate
list.description: Method to be called when this entity is added to a Game. Override in subclass

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onDestroy
list.description: Method called when this entity is destroyed

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onDrawBegin
list.description: Method called each frame before drawing any images of this entity

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onDrawEnd
list.description: Method called each frame after drawing all images of this entity

list.description: Method to process an External Control Protocol event

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onECPKeyboard

list.description: General purpose event handler for in-game events.

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onInput
list.description: Method to process input per frame

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onPause
list.description: Called when the game pauses

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onResume
list.description: Called when the game unpauses

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onUpdate
list.description: Method for handling any updates based on time since previous frame

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#onUrlEvent
list.description: Called on url event

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#removeCollider
list.description: Removes a collider by its name

list.title: BGE.GameEntity#removeImage
list.description: Removes an image from the entity

list.title: BGE/Math.getThirdRightTrianglePoint
list.description:
Gets third point in triangle

?-----B | / | | / | |a/c | A ----| <--- this point is grid aligned, point "?" could be anywhere

list.title: BGE/Math.intersectPointPoint
list.description: From https://paulbourke.net/geometry/pointlineplane/javascript.txt

list.title: BGE/Math/Matrix44.inverse
list.description: Compute the inverse of the matrix using the Gauss-Jordan (or reduced row) elimination method. We didn't explain in the lesson on Geometry how the inverse of matrix can be found. Don't worry at this point if you don't understand how this works. But we will need to be able to compute the inverse of matrices in the first lessons of the "Foundation of 3D Rendering" section, which is why we've added this code. For now, you can just use it and rely on it for doing what it's supposed to do. If you want to learn how this works though, check the lesson on called Matrix Inverse in the "Mathematics and Physics of Computer Graphics" section.

list.title: BGE/Math/Matrix44.invert
list.description: Set current matrix to its inverse

list.title: BGE/Math/Matrix44.multDirMatrix
list.description: This method needs to be used for vector-matrix multiplication. Look at the differences with the previous method (to compute a point-matrix multiplication). We don't use the coefficients in the matrix that account for translation (x[3][0], x[3][1], x[3][2]) and we don't compute w.

list.title: BGE/Math/Matrix44.multVecMatrix
list.description: The coordinate w more often than not equals 1, but it can be different than 1 especially when the matrix is projective matrix (perspective projection matrix).

list.title: BGE/Math/Matrix44.transposed
list.description: Returns the transposed version of this Matrix

list.title: BGE/Math/VectorOps.copy
list.description: Makes a copy of this vector

list.title: BGE/Math/VectorOps.length
list.description: Gets the length of a vector

list.title: BGE/Math/VectorOps.manhattanLength
list.description: Gets the manhattan length of a vector

list.title: BGE/Math/VectorOps.norm
list.description: Gets the square of the length of a vector

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.QuickHull
list.description: Implementation of the QuickHull algorithm for finding convex hull of a set of points Modified from: https://github.com/claytongulick/quickhull Original author Clay Gulick

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.addSegments
list.description: Recursively adds hull segments

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.distalPoints
list.description: Determines the set of points that lay outside the line (positive), and the most distal point Returns: {points: [ [x1, y1], ... ], max: [x,y] ]

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.distanceFromLine
list.description: Calculates the distance of a point from a line modified from https:'github.com/claytongulick/quickhull return {float}

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.getMaxHeightAndVerticalOffset
list.description: Gets the total height from first point to last point vertically, and the offset of the first point

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.getMaxWidthAndHorizontalOffset
list.description: Gets the total width from first point to last point horizontally, and the offset of the first point

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.getMinMaxPoints
list.description: Gets the min and max points in the set along the X axis modified from https:'github.com/claytongulick/quickhull

list.title: BGE/QuickHull.getTrianglesFromPoints
list.description: Gets a series of triangles for the convex space defined by an array of {x,y} points

list.title: rodash
list.description: Adds two numbers.

list.title: rodash/internal.nodeToAA
list.description: Attempts to converts a nodes top level fields to an AssociativeArray. /

list.title: rodash/internal.numberToString
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value to a string. /

list.title: rodash_add
list.description: Adds two numbers.

list.title: rodash_asDateString
list.description: Returns a formatted version of the current time/date.

list.title: rodash_asSeconds
list.description: Returns the current time in seconds.

list.title: rodash_assign
list.description: Assigns own enumerable string keyed properties of source objects to the destination object. Source objects are applied from left to right. Subsequent sources overwrite property assignments of previous sources. This method mutates object and is loosely based on Object.assign.

list.title: rodash_at
list.description: Creates an array of values corresponding to paths of object.

list.title: rodash_ceil
list.description: Computes number rounded up to precision. /

list.title: rodash_chunk
list.description: Creates an array of elements split into groups the length of size. If array can't be split evenly, the final chunk will be the remaining elements. /

list.title: rodash_clone
list.description: Clones objects that can be cloned. /

list.title: rodash_compact
list.description: Creates an array with all falsey values removed. The values false, 0, "", and invalid are falsey. /

list.title: rodash_concat
list.description: Creates a new array concatenating array with any additional arrays and/or values. /

list.title: rodash_difference
list.description: Creates an array of array values not included in the other given arrays using SameValueZero for equality comparisons. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array. /

list.title: rodash_differenceBy
list.description: This method is like rodash.difference except that it accepts iteratee which is invoked for each element of array and values to generate the criterion by which they're compared. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array. The iteratee is invoked with one argument:(value). /

list.title: rodash_differenceWith
list.description: This method is like rodash.difference except that it accepts comparator which is invoked to compare elements of array to values. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array. The comparator is invoked with two arguments: (arrVal, othVal).

list.title: rodash_divide
list.description: Divides two numbers /

list.title: rodash_drop
list.description: Creates a slice of array with n elements dropped from the beginning. /

list.title: rodash_dropRight
list.description: Creates a slice of array with n elements dropped from the end. /

list.title: rodash_dropRightWhile
list.description: Creates a slice of array excluding elements dropped from the end. Elements are dropped until predicate returns falsey. The predicate is invoked with three arguments: (value, index, array). /

list.title: rodash_dropWhile
list.description: Creates a slice of array excluding elements dropped from the beginning. Elements are dropped until predicate returns falsey. The predicate is invoked with three arguments: (value, index, array). /

list.title: rodash_fill
list.description: Fills elements of array with value from start up to, but not including, end. This method mutates array. /

list.title: rodash_findIndex
list.description: This method is like rodash.find except that it returns the index of the first element predicate returns truthy for instead of the element itself. /

list.title: rodash_findLastIndex
list.description: This method is like rodash.findIndex except that it iterates over elements of collection from right to left. /

list.title: rodash_first
list.description: An alias to the head function. /

list.title: rodash_flatten
list.description: Flattens array a single level deep. /

list.title: rodash_flattenDeep
list.description: Recursively flattens array. /

list.title: rodash_flattenDepth
list.description: Recursively flatten array up to depth times. /

list.title: rodash_floor
list.description: Computes number rounded down to precision /

list.title: rodash_forEach
list.description: Iterates over elements of collection and invokes iteratee for each element. The iteratee is invoked with three arguments: (value, index|key, collection). Iteratee functions may exit iteration early by explicitly returning false. Note: As with other "Collections" methods, objects with a "length" property are iterated like arrays. To avoid this behavior use rodash.forIn or rodash.forOwn for object iteration. /

list.title: rodash_forEachRight
list.description: This method is like rodash.forEach except that it iterates over elements of collection from right to left. /

list.title: rodash_forIn
list.description: Iterates over own and inherited enumerable string keyed properties of an object and invokes iteratee for each property. The iteratee is invoked with three arguments: (value, key, object). Iteratee functions may exit iteration early by explicitly returning false. /

list.title: rodash_forInRight
list.description: This method is like rodash.forIn except that it iterates over properties of object in the opposite order. /

list.title: rodash_forOwn
list.description: Iterates over own enumerable string keyed properties of an object and invokes iteratee for each property. The iteratee is invoked with three arguments: (value, key, object). Iteratee functions may exit iteration early by explicitly returning false. /

list.title: rodash_forOwnRight
list.description: This method is like rodash.forOwn except that it iterates over properties of object in the opposite order. /

list.title: rodash_functions
list.description: Creates an array of function property names from own enumerable properties of object. /

list.title: rodash_functionsIn
list.description: Creates an array of function property names from own and inherited enumerable properties of object. /

list.title: rodash_get
list.description: Gets the value at path of object. If the resolved value is undefined, the defaultValue is returned in its place. /

list.title: rodash_hasKeys
list.description: Checks if first level of the supplied AssociativeArray contains the Array of key strings. /

list.title: rodash_head
list.description: Gets the first element of array. /

list.title: rodash_indexOf
list.description: Gets the index at which the first occurrence of value is found in array using SameValueZero for equality comparisons. If fromIndex is negative, it's used as the offset from the end of array. /

list.title: rodash_initial
list.description: Gets all but the last element of array. /

list.title: rodash_internal_nodeToAA
list.description: Attempts to converts a nodes top level fields to an AssociativeArray. /

list.title: rodash_internal_numberToString
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value to a string. /

list.title: rodash_intersection
list.description: Creates an array of unique values that are included in all given arrays using SameValueZero for equality comparisons. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array.

list.title: rodash_intersectionBy
list.description: This method is like rodash.intersection except that it accepts iteratee which is invoked for each element of each arrays to generate the criterion by which they're compared. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array. The iteratee is invoked with one argument:(value).

list.title: rodash_intersectionWith
list.description: This method is like rodash.intersection except that it accepts comparator which is invoked to compare elements of arrays. The order and references of result values are determined by the first array. The comparator is invoked with two arguments: (arrVal, othVal).

list.title: rodash_isAA
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid AssociativeArray type /

list.title: rodash_isArray
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Array type /

list.title: rodash_isArrayLike
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid unempty Array like type /

list.title: rodash_isBoolean
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Boolean type /

list.title: rodash_isByteArray
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid ByteArray type /

list.title: rodash_isDate
list.description: Alias to isDate function /

list.title: rodash_isDateTime
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid date time type /

list.title: rodash_isDouble
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Double type /

list.title: rodash_isElement
list.description: Alias to isNode function /

list.title: rodash_isEmptyString
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid String type and is not empty /

list.title: rodash_isEqual
list.description: Checks if the supplied values are the same. /

list.title: rodash_isEqualWith
list.description: Checks if the supplied values are the same. /

list.title: rodash_isError
list.description: Assesses the passed object to determine if it is an Error Object. / TODO: MORE SUPPORT - TRY/CATCH?

list.title: rodash_isFloat
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Float type /

list.title: rodash_isFunction
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Function type /

list.title: rodash_isInteger
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Integer type /

list.title: rodash_isInvalid
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is Invalid /

list.title: rodash_isLength
list.description: Checks if value is a valid array-like length /

list.title: rodash_isMap
list.description: Alias to isArray function /

list.title: rodash_isNaN
list.description: Method determines whether the passed value is NaN and its type is a valid number /

list.title: rodash_isNode
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Node type /

list.title: rodash_isNonEmptyAA
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid and populated AssociativeArray type /

list.title: rodash_isNonEmptyArray
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid Array type and not empty /

list.title: rodash_isNonEmptyString
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid String type and is not empty /

list.title: rodash_isNotInvalid
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is not Invalid or uninitialized /

list.title: rodash_isNull
list.description: Alias to isInvalid function /

list.title: rodash_isNumber
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid number type

list.title: rodash_isString
list.description: Checks if the supplied value is a valid String type /

list.title: rodash_join
list.description: Converts all elements in array into a string separated by separator. /

list.title: rodash_last
list.description: Gets the last element of array. /

list.title: rodash_map
list.description: Creates an array of values by running each element in collection thru iteratee. The iteratee is invoked with three arguments:(value, index|key, collection) /

list.title: rodash_max
list.description: Computes the maximum value of array. If array is empty or falsey, invalid is returned. /

list.title: rodash_maxBy
list.description: Computes the maximum value of array. If array is empty or falsey, invalid is returned. /

list.title: rodash_min
list.description: Computes the minimum value of array. If array is empty or falsey, invalid is returned. /

list.title: rodash_minBy
list.description: Computes the minimum value of array. If array is empty or falsey, invalid is returned. /

list.title: rodash_nodeToString
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value to a string.

list.title: rodash_paddString
list.description: Add padding to the supplied value after converting to a string. For example "1" to "01". /

list.title: rodash_reduce
list.description: Reduces collection to a value which is the accumulated result of running each element in collection thru iteratee, where each successive invocation is supplied the return value of the previous. If accumulator is not given, the first element of collection is used as the initial value. The iteratee is invoked with four arguments:(accumulator, value, index|key, collection). /

list.title: rodash_set
list.description: Used to set a nested String value in the supplied object /

list.title: rodash_shuffle
list.description: Creates an array of shuffled values, using a version of the Fisher-Yates shuffle. /

list.title: rodash_size
list.description: Gets the size of collection by returning its length for array-like values or the number of own enumerable string keyed properties for objects. /

list.title: rodash_slice
list.description: Creates a slice of array from start up to, but not including, end. /

list.title: rodash_sortBy
list.description: Creates an array of elements, sorted in ascending order by the results of running each element in a collection thru each iteratee. This method performs a stable sort, that is, it preserves the original sort order of equal elements. The iteratees are invoked with one argument: (value). /

list.title: rodash_sortedIndex
list.description: Uses a binary search to determine the lowest index at which value should be inserted into array in order to maintain its sort order. /

list.title: rodash_stringIncludes
list.description: Check for the existence of a given sub string /

list.title: rodash_stringIndexOf
list.description: Finds the sub string index position /

list.title: rodash_subtract
list.description: Subtract two numbers. /

list.title: rodash_take
list.description: Creates a slice of array with n elements taken from the beginning /

list.title: rodash_takeRight
list.description: Creates a slice of array with n elements taken from the end /

list.title: rodash_toArray
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value to a array.

list.title: rodash_toNumber
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value into a valid number /

list.title: rodash_toString
list.description: Attempts to convert the supplied value to a string. /

list.title: rodash_union
list.description: Creates an array of unique values, in order, from all given arrays using SameValueZero for equality comparisons. /

list.title: rodash_uniq
list.description: Creates a duplicate-free version of an array, using SameValueZero for equality comparisons, in which only the first occurrence of each element is kept. The order of result values is determined by the order they occur in the array. /

list.title: rodash_zip
list.description: Creates an array of grouped elements, the first of which contains the first elements of the given arrays, the second of which contains the second elements of the given arrays, and so on. /

list.title: rodash_zipObject
list.description: This method is like rodash.fromPairs except that it accepts two arrays, one of property identifiers and one of corresponding values.

list.title: rodash_zipObjectDeep
list.description: This method is like rodash.zipObject except that it supports property paths.
